perturbation.original_text: In consideration for receiving certain confidential information in connection with provid ing services to or on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers (CCPDT), I, herein referred to as “Volunteer”, hereby agree and acknowledge:
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration for receiving certain confidential information in connection with providing services to or on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers (CCPDT), I, herein referred to as “Contractor”, hereby agree and acknowledge:
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Volunteer' to 'Contractor' in this section introduces ambiguity. Throughout the document, 'Volunteer' is used consistently to define the party bound by the agreement. This change creates confusion about whether 'Contractor' is now a distinct legal entity also subject to the agreement, or if the terms are interchangeable. This is directly contradicting the first section and following sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for receiving certain confidential information in connection with providing services to or on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers (CCPDT), I, herein referred to as “Contractor”, hereby agree and acknowledge: Confidential and Proprietary Information For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential I nformation” means all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer, or to which Volunteer otherwise g ains access as a result of volunteering for the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, pertaining in any manner to the activities of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any perso n or entity to which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential. All proprietary information of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers that is not known generally to the public is Confidential Information. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the following are deemed Confidential Information:  Ideas for research and development;  Information submitted in the investig ation of complaints or involving ethics cases;  Computer records and software (including software that is proprietary to third parties);  Any other information which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers must keep confidential as a result of obligations to third parties;  Information regarding the administration of components of the certification programs;  Exam -related technologies and components;  Item content, characteristics, development or other aspects of the examinations and their de velopment, maintenance and administration;  Identities of Certificants, candidates, customers, suppliers, or third party contractors, including without limitation any media, advertising, or public relations firms;  The Certification Council for Professiona l Dog Trainers’ e -mail distribution list(s);  Human resources data and information about employees, contractors and other volunteers;  Cost and other financial data;  Polling and focus group information;  Any goods or services Volunteer provides to the Cer tification Council for Professional Dog Trainers under this Agreement.  Any other information to which Volunteer has access while involved in the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ activities.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the individual as "Volunteer," while the changed text redefines this individual as "Contractor." The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Volunteer' when defining confidential information and their obligations. The change introduces a direct contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'Volunteer' throughout, while the changed text introduces 'Contractor,' creating ambiguity and conflicting obligations as to whom the agreement applies.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential I nformation” means all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer, or to which Volunteer otherwise g ains access as a result of volunteering for the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, pertaining in any manner to the activities of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any perso n or entity to which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Restricted Data” means all information and materials, in whatever form, whether tangible or intangible, disclosed by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or any of its authorized representatives to Volunteer, or to which Volunteer otherwise gains access as a result of volunteering for the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, pertaining in any manner to the activities of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers or its affiliates, consultants, members, or any person or entity to which the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers owes a duty of confidentiality, whether or not labeled or identified as proprietary or confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Restricted Data' in the definition section creates a contradiction. The rest of the document refers to 'Confidential Information'. This change means that sections referring to 'Confidential Information' may not apply to the information defined as 'Restricted Data,' causing uncertainty about what information is actually protected. This change is contradicting the definition of what the volunteer's duties are to protect such confidential information.
perturbation.location: Section: Confidential and Proprietary Information, Paragraph: 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure Volunteer agrees not to, wi thout prior written consent from the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers, divulge any Confidential Information to third parties or copy documents containing any Confidential Information. In no event shall Volunteer use Confidential Informat ion in a manner that is in any way detrimental to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information,' and the contradicted text uses this definition. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Restricted Data' in the definition section, but the contradicted section still refers to 'Confidential Information.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted section now lacks a clear definition of what 'Confidential Information' refers to, leading to uncertainty about the volunteer's obligations, thus creating a misaligned terminology contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall act fai rly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and to the population of Certificants and candidates.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers should act fai rly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and to the population of Certificants and candidates.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'should' weakens the obligation on the volunteers, potentially making them no longer obligated to act a certain way, making the code of conduct of the volunteers optional instead of required. This creates uncertainty and contradiction with other sections expecting volunteers to act under this requirement.
perturbation.location: Section: Volunteer Code of Conduct, Paragraph: 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Section: Volunteer Code of Conduct, Paragraph: 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteers should act fai rly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and to the population of Certificants and candidates. Volunteers shall conduct themselves in a professional manner during the conduct of certific ation business and shall treat all stakeholders with courtesy and respect. Volunteers shall not speak or act on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers without proper written authorization from the CCPDT. Volunteers shall support the decisions of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. Volunteers shall participate in the activities required of them to fulfill their charge(s) and serve the mission of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. Volunt eers shall be an advocate for the CCPDT’s certification programs and support its mission. Volunteers shall acknowledge that failure to uphold and abide by the Volunteer Code of Conduct may result in removal from participation in the certification program and volunteers shall willingly accept their termination as discussed in the next section.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "shall," creating a mandatory obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to "should," making it advisory. The contradicted text expects volunteers to act according to a defined code of conduct, with consequences for failing to do so. By weakening the initial 'shall' to 'should', the enforcement of the code of conduct and the consequences become ambiguous, directly conflicting with the expectation of mandatory compliance outlined in the contradicted text. This meets the definition of a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as it leads to conflicting obligations.
